summary:
Anodontia primarily influences the development and growth of the alveolar bones within both the maxillary and mandibular regions due to its direct impact on tooth presence and their supportive structures during embryonic stages. This condition does not significantly alter cranial or overall jawbone dimensions beyond these specific sites related directly to missing teeth. The other options are less pertinent as they do not have a primary role in supporting absent dentition. ```plaintext

Anodontia mainly affects the growth of the alveolar bones in the maxilla and mandible by impacting tooth-supporting tissues; craniomaxillofacial structures outside these areas remain largely unaffected.
```
